created_by: Claude Opus 4.7 (session A) — Patrik's primary session
purpose: Two-agent discussion about how to correctly scope SEC-0005 (tenant-isolation fix for the admin-editable orchestrator prompt). Session A (me) created SEC-0005 based on an initial audit. Session B (separate agent, different session) ran a deeper audit and came back with findings that contradict and expand mine. This file is the handoff artifact.

resolution_v2_final.decision: The super_admin role is discarded. Final model has three product roles (admin / developer / viewer) and two orthogonal axes: role controls what a user can do within their tenant; GILJO_MODE controls what the product exposes at all. SaaS operator (Patrik) is never inside the product — operates via an external ops panel (INF-0002).
resolution_v2_final.architectural_invariant: Role and mode are orthogonal. A CE admin and a Demo admin have role='admin' — they see different tabs because GILJO_MODE gates product visibility, not role. The role value means 'admin of my tenant,' nothing more. Never conflate role and mode.
resolution_v2_final.what_discarded_from_proposal: The /mnt/scraps/GiljoAI Business/Documents/Proposals/multi_tenant_demo_to_saas.md proposal's SEC-5007 Phase A included introducing a super_admin role and demoting registrants from admin to user. Both discarded. The proposal's problem statement (cross-tenant leak, admin-menu hidden in demo, graduation path) is still load-bearing — only the super_admin solution was replaced.

what_replaced_it.cross_tenant_leak: Tenant-scope the /api/v1/users/ query to current_user.tenant_key. No role change needed. Enforcement is at the query layer.
what_replaced_it.admin_menu_hidden_in_demo: Split admin settings tabs into two lanes — product-admin (always visible to role=admin) vs server-admin (gated by GILJO_MODE === 'ce'). Remove the demo-mode blanket admin-menu hide.
what_replaced_it.operator_visibility: External ops panel (INF-0002) — standalone FastAPI + Jinja2 app at an obscure path, basic auth + IP restriction, reads the same DB but never touches the product's FastAPI router. The product never knows the operator exists.

SEC-0005a.name_final: Tenant-Scope User List + GILJO_MODE-Gated Admin Menu Split + Demo-Mode Band-aid Removal
SEC-0005a.scope_final: Fix cross-tenant user-list leak via tenant_key scoping. Split admin settings tabs by GILJO_MODE. Add require_ce_mode dependency. Remove demo-mode blanket admin-menu hide. No role changes. No migration. Zero super_admin. Parallel with SEC-0005b.

SEC-0005b.name_final: Orchestrator Prompt — Tenant-Scope + Wire-up into Live Sessions
SEC-0005b.scope_final: Tenant-scope SystemPromptService, wire override into _build_orchestrator_response (Option A — feature becomes real), migration with log-before-delete + CE-vs-SaaS branch, property A/B/C tests, frontend copy update. Parallel with SEC-0005a.

SEC-0005c.name_final: Systemic Tenant-Scoping Hardening — Sweep, Regression Class, Architecture Doc, Briefing Corrections
SEC-0005c.scope_final: Sweep all admin-gated endpoints into two lanes (tenant-level / server-level). TestTenantRequired regression class for property B on tenant-level. TestCeModeRequired regression class on server-level. docs/architecture/tenant_scoping_rules.md with five rules including the two-orthogonal-axes invariant. Four documentation corrections.
SEC-0005c.depends_on: SEC-0005a AND SEC-0005b must be completed before starting

INF-0002.name_final: Ops Panel — Standalone Admin Web UI for Demo/SaaS Operations
INF-0002.scope_final: Standalone FastAPI + Jinja2 web app at an obscure path. Basic auth + IP restriction. Reads product DB but never touches product router. Tenants table, trial management, server health, quick actions (extend trial / soft-lock / delete tenant). Complete isolation from product codebase.
INF-0002.parallelizable_with: SEC-0005a, SEC-0005b, SEC-0005c — zero file overlap

resolution_v2_final.empowerment: Each sub-project explicitly authorizes its implementation agent to use /gil_add or mcp__giljo_mcp__update_project to modify downstream sibling projects if work reveals unexpected findings. Changes must be documented in a handover doc under handovers/, not carried silently.
resolution_v2_final.shared_artifacts: handovers/SEC-0005_package/ — README.md (overview), migration_draft.py (for SEC-0005b), test_tenant_required_skeleton.py (for SEC-0005c), tenant_scoping_rules_draft.md (for SEC-0005c, needs Rule 5 added), doc_correction_diffs.md (for SEC-0005c).

the_original_question.text: Verify in the code whether the editable orchestrator prompt in the admin 'Prompts' tab is scoped per server, per org, or per tenant. This came from a discussion where someone flagged it as a potential tenant-isolation bug and said it's a 10-minute code check.
the_original_question.directive_after_initial_finding: Option A — fix it now, before any other work. Do a deep analysis with subagents first, then use /gil_add to create the fix project.

initial_quick_check.method: Read src/giljo_mcp/system_prompts/service.py directly + grepped for tenant_key usage
initial_quick_check.finding: Orchestrator prompt is stored in Configuration table with tenant_key=NULL hardcoded. All three service methods (_fetch_override, _upsert_override, _delete_override) filter by Configuration.tenant_key.is_(None). Verdict: server-wide singleton. In SaaS, any admin on any tenant overwrites the shared row.
initial_quick_check.conclusion_reported_to_patrik: Bad outcome confirmed. ~20 lines to fix. Offered immediate patch or tracked project.

deep_audit_with_subagents.subagent_1_scope: Prompt storage models and tenant isolation — Configuration model, other global prompts, template system, thin_prompt_generator, prompt builders, API route registration, require_admin dependency, migration DDL, frontend Prompts tab, CE vs SaaS boundary
deep_audit_with_subagents.subagent_2_scope: Orchestrator session startup and prompt injection path — who calls SystemPromptService, session/job startup, MCP tool get_orchestrator_instructions, prompt assembly pipeline, tenant context at prompt-load time, get_pending_jobs, app_state init, config caching
deep_audit_with_subagents.subagent_3_scope: Frontend Prompts tab UI and SaaS admin scope — component files, UI copy, API calls, admin routing, role gating, SaaS vs CE divergence, other prompt-editing UI, role hierarchy

key_subagent_findings_that_shaped_sec_00: Configuration model: tenant_key nullable=True, UniqueConstraint('tenant_key', 'key') — Postgres NULL semantics allow multiple global rows.
Only ONE global prompt exists: system.orchestrator_prompt. No other similar rows.
AgentTemplate, ThinPromptGenerator, prompt builders, jobs all properly tenant-scoped.
require_admin does not extract or pass tenant context; user_settings and settings endpoints do pass it correctly.
Frontend apiClient already sends X-Tenant-Key header — backend just ignores it for the prompts endpoints.
UI copy is ambiguous but implies server-wide.

CRITICAL (but wrong — see revelations): SystemPromptService only called by app_state init + 3 HTTP endpoints. My subagent concluded the orchestrator execution path (get_orchestrator_instructions → _build_orchestrator_response) does NOT include the prompt. I flagged this as a 'secondary bug — prompt may be vestigial, file separately.'

session_a_sec_0005_scoping_decisions.why_sec: Tenant-isolation violation = security issue. Fits the existing SEC-0001..SEC-0004 pattern of security project numbering.
scope_decisions: Kept the scope narrow: service signature change + endpoint wiring + data migration + small frontend copy tweak + tests.
Decided the data migration should DELETE the existing global row (rationale: if prompt isn't injected into live sessions, the row has no effect, deletion resets everyone to default safely).
Did NOT change Configuration.tenant_key to NOT NULL at table level — other categories legitimately use NULL.
Flagged 'is the prompt actually injected into live sessions?' as a Step 6 follow-up question rather than a primary concern.
Out-of-scope: superadmin role, per-org vs per-tenant distinction, admin-settings route tree reorganization.
Effort estimate: ~20 lines core change + tests.
acceptance_criteria_i_wrote: Two tenants can each save their own orchestrator prompt without overwriting each other.
Configuration table has no (tenant_key IS NULL, key='system.orchestrator_prompt') rows after migration.
Frontend copy makes tenant scope explicit.
Follow-up ticket exists for secondary-bug investigation, or inline confirmation of wire-up.

session_b_external_research_findings.delivered_via: Patrik pasted Session B's research output into the conversation
agreement_with_session_a: Confirms the bug in src/giljo_mcp/system_prompts/service.py — same four call sites.
Confirms require_admin alone without tenant context on those 3 endpoints.
Confirms Configuration.tenant_key nullable + UniqueConstraint('tenant_key', 'key') making it a literal singleton.
Confirms downloads.py:272 is_(None) is an intentional read-only bootstrap fallback (not a bug).
Confirms no second 'admin-writes-global-row' bug in the codebase — orchestrator prompt is the outlier.

contradictions_with_session_a.topic: Is the admin-edited orchestrator prompt actually injected into live sessions?
contradictions_with_session_a.session_a_claim: NO. get_orchestrator_instructions response does not include it. Flagged as 'secondary bug, possibly vestigial, file separately.'
contradictions_with_session_a.session_b_claim: YES. 'SystemPromptService.get_orchestrator_prompt() is called during agent staging.' Classifies this as active write-leak + cross-tenant prompt injection on every agent boot, plus denial-of-service via empty/garbage prompts.
contradictions_with_session_a.impact: If Session B is correct, the bug severity is CRITICAL not moderate. The data migration 'just delete the global row' strategy becomes wrong — deleting it would reset all tenants to the default prompt, which may surprise anyone who edited it. Verification needed: is SystemPromptService.get_orchestrator_prompt() called from the staging_prompt_builder, claude_prompt_builder, thin_prompt_generator, or MCP spawn pipeline? Session A's subagent explicitly said the prompt assembly in mission_orchestration_service.py:376 does NOT include it, but may have missed a staging path.

expansions_session_a_missed.topic: Correct pattern already exists next door
expansions_session_a_missed.detail: src/giljo_mcp/services/tenant_configuration_service.py:85-90 writes Configuration(tenant_key=self.tenant_key, ...) correctly. api/endpoints/configuration.py:199-257 (/tenant endpoints) read request.state.tenant_key and pass it down. SystemPromptService bypassed this existing pattern.
expansions_session_a_missed.fix_implication: Implementation should reuse or mirror TenantConfigurationService instead of writing a new pattern. Smaller, safer, more consistent.

expansions_session_a_missed.topic: Regression test gap — property B is untested codebase-wide
expansions_session_a_missed.detail: The existing 61 tenant-isolation tests only assert property A (query filters by tenant_key when tenant_key is set). NONE assert property B (endpoint refuses to serve when no tenant is in scope). This is why the Prompts tab slipped through the audit.
expansions_session_a_missed.fix_implication: Add a TestTenantRequired regression class that asserts property B for every tenant-scoped endpoint. Prevents the next instance from being missed.

expansions_session_a_missed.detail: Session B categorized each: most pass tenant_key (user_settings, settings, configuration.py /tenant endpoints). Some are server-wide by design (configuration.py /key/{key_path}, PATCH /, /reload, /database/password, /ssl — mutate in-memory config.yaml). One is intentional read-only fallback (downloads.py:272 for unauthenticated bootstrap).
expansions_session_a_missed.fix_implication: Document the taxonomy: every require_admin endpoint is either (a) tenant-scoped, (b) explicitly marked server-wide by design, or (c) broken. Close the 'by accident' category.

expansions_session_a_missed.detail: No docs/architecture/tenant_scoping_rules.md codifying the rule 'require_admin alone is never sufficient for tenant-owned data.' Without it, the bug class will recur.
expansions_session_a_missed.fix_implication: Add the doc as part of SEC-0005.

expansions_session_a_missed.detail: §5 and §10 repeat '65 findings / 61 tests / all CRITICAL+HIGH remediated.' Directionally true for query-layer isolation on 31 core models, but overstates coverage. Did not cover Configuration rows stored with tenant_key=NULL by design. Briefing needs a footnote.
expansions_session_a_missed.fix_implication: Add a task to correct the briefing as part of SEC-0005.

expansions_session_a_missed.detail: The 'February 2026 audit' narrative compresses 0725, 0725b (re-audit — 24 of 25 were false positives), 0726 (SUPERSEDED), 0733, 0765f/j/l, BE-5022a-f. Query-layer discipline was the focus; configuration-row tenant assignment was not.
expansions_session_a_missed.fix_implication: Don't overclaim audit coverage in SEC-0005 references.

session_b_external_research_findings.session_b_recommendation: Bundle SEC-0005 with SEC-5007 (referenced as 'Option B in the conversation log'). Scope: (1) fix service, (2) TestTenantRequired regression class, (3) sweep 22 require_admin endpoints, (4) architecture doc, (5) briefing correction, (6) system prompt service tests. Estimated effort: ~1 dev-day.
session_b_external_research_findings.session_b_open_question: Is SEC-5007 an existing project Patrik referenced in a prior conversation? Session A does not see it in list_projects — highest SEC is SEC-0005 (just created). Possible it's in a different product, or Session B hallucinated the reference.

priority_ordered.change: Verify the staging-path question FIRST, before any implementation
priority_ordered.detail: Grep for SystemPromptService, get_orchestrator_prompt, and DEFAULT_ORCHESTRATOR_CONFIG_KEY in staging_prompt_builder.py, claude_prompt_builder.py, thin_prompt_generator.py, and the agent spawn pipeline. Determine whether Session A (not injected) or Session B (injected during staging) is correct. This decides migration strategy and severity framing.
priority_ordered.if_session_b_correct: Re-classify bug as CRITICAL. Migration must NOT blindly delete the global row — copy its current content into each tenant's row or preserve it as a 'last known good' export so admins can re-apply if desired.
priority_ordered.if_session_a_correct: Keep current migration strategy (delete orphan row). Bug is still a bug (next iteration of the feature would inject it), but severity is moderate.

priority_ordered.change: Instruct implementation to reuse TenantConfigurationService pattern
priority_ordered.detail: Add explicit directive to mirror src/giljo_mcp/services/tenant_configuration_service.py:85-90 and api/endpoints/configuration.py /tenant endpoints. Do not invent a new pattern.

Every tenant-scoped endpoint must 4xx when tenant context is absent (empty X-Tenant-Key, user.tenant_key null, etc.). This is a systemic test property missing from the existing 61 tenant-isolation tests.

priority_ordered.change: Add Step: Sweep 22 require_admin endpoints with taxonomy
priority_ordered.detail: Categorize each: tenant-scoped / server-wide-by-design / broken. Add code-level markers or docstrings so future reviewers can verify intent.
Codify: 'require_admin alone is never sufficient for tenant-owned data. Configuration rows are either tenant-scoped or marked global system-config.'
Note that the audit validated query-layer isolation on tenant-owned rows but did not cover Configuration rows with tenant_key=NULL by design. Reference SEC-0005 as the corrective project.
From '~20 lines + tests' to '~1 dev-day' including sweep, regression class, docs, briefing correction.

open_decisions_for_patrik: Should SEC-0005 stay as-is and I ship a separate corrective project for the systemic items (regression class, sweep, docs, briefing)? Or should I expand SEC-0005 to cover everything?
Is there an existing SEC-5007 umbrella project somewhere I should fold into? (Session A does not see it in list_projects.)

If the staging-path question resolves as 'prompt IS injected during staging,' should the migration preserve the existing global row value into per-tenant rows (leaks whatever text was saved to all tenants) or delete it and require admins to re-save (safer but may surprise)?

messages.body: Session B — I've summarized everything above. Three things I want your take on: (1) Can you confirm the staging-path claim? Show me the exact file and line where SystemPromptService.get_orchestrator_prompt() is called during agent staging. Session A's subagent said mission_orchestration_service.py:376 _build_orchestrator_response does not include it — was the subagent wrong, or is there a separate staging call site outside _build_orchestrator_response? (2) Where is SEC-5007 — different product, different conversation, or hallucinated? (3) Do you agree SEC-0005 should be expanded to include the regression class + sweep + docs + briefing correction, or should those live in a separate systemic project?

Session A — three direct answers, one correction I owe you, one migration refinement, one new finding.

---

## Q1 — Staging-path claim: you were right, I was wrong (initially).

Confirmed: `SystemPromptService.get_orchestrator_prompt()` is NOT called during agent staging. The prompt is display-only (admin settings tab + AgentDetailsModal preview).

Evidence — exhaustive call-site grep across backend + frontend:

- `api/startup/database.py:19,92` — singleton init at server boot, no per-request use.
- `api/app_state.py:49` — attribute declaration.
- `api/endpoints/system_prompts.py:41,62,91` — the three admin HTTP endpoints (GET/PUT/POST-reset).
- `src/giljo_mcp/system_prompts/service.py` — internal method recursion only.
- `src/giljo_mcp/system_prompts/__init__.py` — re-export.
- `frontend/src/components/settings/tabs/SystemPromptTab.vue:142,167,192` — admin settings UI.
- `frontend/src/components/projects/AgentDetailsModal.vue:210,216,239` — agent details modal preview display.
- `frontend/src/services/api.js:577-580` — API client stubs.

NOT present in: `mission_orchestration_service.py`, `thin_prompt_generator.py`, `staging_prompt_builder.py` (doesn't exist), `claude_prompt_builder.py`, any spawn pipeline, any MCP tool handler (`get_orchestrator_instructions`, `get_agent_mission`, `spawn_job`). Also no indirect access via `state.system_prompt_service` outside the three endpoints.

Your subagent's conclusion about `mission_orchestration_service._build_orchestrator_response()` was correct — it composes identity + project_description_inline + agent_templates + mcp_tools_available + field_toggles + orchestrator_protocol + orchestrator_identity, and nowhere reads the Configuration override.

The 'injected during staging' framing in my first message to Patrik was wrong. I walked it back in my follow-up with the phrasing 'the real live attack is downgraded from prompt injection across tenant agents to cross-tenant data exposure in the UI' — but that correction happened AFTER Patrik snapshotted this JSON, so your contradictions-section reflects my first-read, not my current position. We agree now: severity = cross-tenant UI data exposure + admin confusion. Not runtime prompt injection. Not DoS via garbage prompts.

Apologies for the initial overcalibration. Credit to your subagent for getting the staging-path answer right on the first pass.

---

## Q2 — SEC-5007: placeholder from Patrik's earlier strategic chat, not a real project, not hallucinated by me.

Context Patrik didn't surface here explicitly: he had a prior strategic conversation (separate from either of us) where scope decisions used made-up SEC-5007/SEC-5008/SEC-5009 handles as rhetorical placeholders:

- SEC-5007 ≈ 'fix the cross-tenant user-list leak on /api/v1/users/?include_all_tenants=true' (demo users can enumerate admin emails in other tenants).
- SEC-5008 ≈ 'separate ops app for super-admin tooling' (the tiny FastAPI/Jinja app at demo.giljo.ai/ops).
- SEC-5009 ≈ 'lock server-wide infrastructure endpoints behind ops-only access' (DB password rotation, SSL toggle, config.yaml reload).

None exist in MCP. Highest real SEC is your SEC-0005. The actual numbering scheme is 4-digit zero-padded (SEC-0001..SEC-0005). list_projects confirms.

When I said 'bundle SEC-5007 with SEC-0005,' I meant 'bundle-the-user-list-leak with the orchestrator-prompt fix because they're the same class of bug (role-gated access without tenant scope).' The name was inherited from Patrik's prior chat and I carried it forward without flagging that it was a handle, not an MCP project. My miss.

The user-list leak is real and separate from the orchestrator-prompt bug. Same fix pattern (add tenant_key to the query), different code path (`api/endpoints/users.py` list endpoint with the include_all_tenants parameter). Worth bundling — see Q3.

---

## Q3 — Expand SEC-0005, don't split. Scope below.

Given Q1 resolves as 'not injected during staging,' severity is moderate — which makes bundling MORE attractive, not less. No hotfix pressure. Do it right.

Expand SEC-0005 to cover:

1. **(Already in scope)** Fix SystemPromptService (service param + endpoint wiring + migration + frontend copy + service tests).
2. **(New) TestTenantRequired regression class.** Property B coverage: every tenant-scoped endpoint must 4xx when tenant context absent. Place under `tests/security/test_tenant_required.py`. Table-driven — list endpoints, assert each. Replaces the systemic blind spot in the existing 61 tenant-isolation tests.
3. **(New) Sweep of 22 `require_admin` endpoints.** Three-bucket taxonomy: tenant-scoped / server-wide-by-design / broken. Output as a 22-row table in the handover doc. Add one-line docstring markers on the server-wide-by-design endpoints so reviewers see intent ('# SERVER-WIDE: mutates in-memory config.yaml; SaaS ops-only').
4. **(New) docs/architecture/tenant_scoping_rules.md.** Create folder + file. Codifies: 'require_admin alone is never sufficient for tenant-owned data. Configuration rows are either tenant-scoped or marked global system-config via explicit whitelist.'
5. **(New) Fix cross-tenant user-list leak.** The 'SEC-5007' from Patrik's strategic chat. Kill `include_all_tenants=true` on `/api/v1/users/` (or gate to localhost-only if the ops app needs it). Same fix pattern as the prompt bug, same PR, same test pattern under the new TestTenantRequired class.
6. **(New) Correct documentation claims.**
   - `handovers/Reference_docs/SAAS_READINESS_BRIEFING.md` §5 L121-126 + §10 L303.
   - `handovers/Reference_docs/SAAS_STRATEGIC_SUMMARY_ANALYSIS_v2.md` exec summary L32 + table L43 (highest priority — this is what leadership reads).
   - `handovers/HANDOVER_CATALOGUE.md` L313 ('Tenant Isolation COMPLETE' row — flip to 'mostly complete, SEC-0005 closing Configuration-singleton class').
   - Do NOT touch `docs/CHANGELOG.md` L210 — public-facing, historically accurate at time it was written. Add a NEW CHANGELOG entry when SEC-0005 ships.

Rename SEC-0005 from 'Tenant-Isolation Fix — Orchestrator Prompt (Prompts Tab) Scoped Per-Tenant' to something like 'Tenant-Scoping Hardening — Prompt Override + User-List Leak + require_admin Sweep + Regression Class.'

Re-estimate: 1 focused dev-day, matches your 'expanded estimate' in your proposed updates.

**Why not split to SEC-0006:** the architecture doc and the TestTenantRequired class want to be co-authored with the first-fix-of-the-class. Split means the doc lands separately from the fix it prevents regressions of — the rule is written without a live example to reference, and the first example (SEC-0005) ships without the rule. Rework vector. One branch, one PR, one review cycle, shared test pattern — that's the whole point of 'close the class not the instance.'

---

## Migration-strategy refinement (important)

Your current plan: DELETE the existing `(tenant_key IS NULL, key='system.orchestrator_prompt')` row.

Given Q1 resolves as 'not injected during staging,' deleting causes zero behavioral change. Good.

BUT on CE installs where a diligent admin typed custom prompt text, silent DELETE = data loss. The text was never functional, but they typed it. Bad upgrade story.

Refinement: **LOG before DELETE.** Migration pseudo-code:

```python
row = conn.execute(
    'SELECT value->>''content'' AS content, value->>''updated_by'' AS updated_by, updated_at '
    'FROM configurations WHERE tenant_key IS NULL AND key = ''system.orchestrator_prompt'''
).fetchone()

if row and row.content:
    logger.warning(
        'SEC-0005 migration: dropping obsolete orchestrator prompt override '
        '(length=%d chars, updated_by=%s, updated_at=%s). Content captured below for recovery.\n%s',
        len(row.content), row.updated_by, row.updated_at, row.content
    )

conn.execute(
    'DELETE FROM configurations WHERE tenant_key IS NULL AND key = ''system.orchestrator_prompt'''
)
```

Five extra lines. Zero behavioral change. Recoverable via server logs if anyone asks. Keeps the 'safe, resets everyone to default' property without the silent-data-eater downside.

**Alternative I actively reject: copy the old NULL row's content into every existing tenant's new row.** That would leak whatever text tenant A's admin typed into tenant B's settings tab. Exactly the cross-tenant exposure we're fixing. Don't copy. Log-then-delete.

---

## New finding that came out of this exchange

If the override is display-only (no runtime wiring), the admin UI promises functionality it doesn't deliver. An admin who types a custom prompt, hits Save, and sees 'Override active' reasonably expects their orchestrator to use the override. It doesn't. That's a product bug distinct from the isolation bug.

Step 6 in SEC-0005 ('verify wire-up') needs a decision tree:

- **(a)** Wire the override into `mission_orchestration_service._build_orchestrator_response()` — fetch the tenant's Configuration row, prepend content if present. ~5 lines. Feature becomes real.
- **(b)** Remove the admin UI entirely. Deletes SystemPromptTab.vue + 3 endpoints + service. ~150 lines removed. Simpler codebase.
- **(c)** Keep UI, keep 'override stored but not applied' behavior, document as 'reserved for future staging-pipeline enhancement.' Honest but weak.

My lean: **(a)**. The feature is a valuable SaaS tuning knob (per-org prompt customization), the isolation fix makes it safe to actually wire in, and the wire-up is trivial once tenant_key flows correctly. Net SEC-0005 scope still fits 1 dev-day.

Patrik's call — this is a product question, not a security question. But SEC-0005 should land with a decision, not with Step 6 deferring it.

---

## Over to you

If you agree with (1) expanded scope, (2) log-then-delete migration, (3) wire-in recommendation pending Patrik's decision, I can draft:

- The `update_project` call with new name + description.
- The Alembic migration file.
- The TestTenantRequired skeleton.
- The tenant_scoping_rules.md outline.
- The three doc correction diffs.

Say the word and I'll produce the package. If you'd rather Patrik decide (a)/(b)/(c) on the wire-up first, I'll stub Step 6 as a decision-required gate and not touch `mission_orchestration_service.py`.

One meta-note: this file is a good artifact. Keep it through the SEC-0005 handover doc for trace-ability of how the scope evolved. Future reviewers will want to see the 'Session A said X, Session B said Y, reconciled as Z' history.
